event: Presentation of Jesus at the Temple
symbols: infant Jesus held by Simeon — the long-awaited Messiah recognized by the righteous elder
blue mantle of Mary — her purity and heavenly grace as Theotokos
white cloth wrapping the Child — sacrificial purity and the swaddling that echoes burial linens
golden ornate apse behind — the holiness of the Temple, the dwelling of God's presence
dove or fruit in the upper medallion — likely the turtledoves prescribed for the purification offering
elderly Anna with covered head — the prophetess who confirmed Jesus as the redemption of Jerusalem
Joseph among the attendants — the faithful guardian fulfilling the Law's requirements
description: A detail from a larger altarpiece showing the Presentation of Jesus at the Temple. At center, the elderly Simeon, bald and bearded, gently holds the nude Christ Child against his chest, gazing at the infant with profound tenderness. The Virgin Mary stands at left in a deep blue mantle over a white veil, her hands extended as she has just handed over the Child. Behind and around the central pair stand several figures: an older woman with a white head-covering (Anna the Prophetess) and two or three bearded men, likely including Joseph.
theological_description: The Presentation at the Temple, recorded in Luke 2:22-40, is one of the most theologically dense events of Christ's infancy. Mary and Joseph bring the forty-day-old Jesus to the Temple in Jerusalem to fulfill the Mosaic Law requiring the consecration of every firstborn male (Exod 13:2) and the mother's purification offering (Lev 12:6-8). The aged Simeon, who had received a divine promise that he would not die before seeing the Lord's Messiah, takes the Child in his arms and utters the Nunc Dimittis: 'Lord, now you are letting your servant depart in peace, according to your word; for my eyes have seen your salvation' (Luke 2:29-30). His prophecy declares Jesus to be 'a light for revelation to the Gentiles, and for glory to your people Israel' (Luke 2:32), establishing the universal scope of salvation. Simeon also warns Mary that 'a sword will pierce through your own soul also' (Luke 2:35), foreshadowing the Passion. The prophetess Anna confirms the messianic identification, giving thanks and speaking of the Child to all who awaited the redemption of Jerusalem. This scene marks the moment when the Old Covenant recognizes and yields to the New — the Temple receives its true Lord.

scripture_refs.reason: The direct biblical account of the Presentation at the Temple, including Simeon's Nunc Dimittis and prophecy to Mary
Anna the Prophetess recognizes the Child and proclaims Him as the redemption of Jerusalem
The consecration of every firstborn to the Lord, the Mosaic law fulfilled in bringing Jesus to the Temple
The purification offering required of mothers after childbirth, which Mary faithfully presents
The Lord whom you seek will suddenly come to His temple — fulfilled when the infant Christ enters the Temple
Simeon's declaration that Christ is a light to the Gentiles echoes Isaiah's Servant Song prophecy
